raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the contribution, framing, and technical point of the section.","R1.2":"The artifact contains a multi-step argument about prediction error, online retuning, tracking performance, and robustness that would need coherent causal logic.","R1.3":"Several performance and robustness claims rely on reported benchmark results, so strengthening requires aligning claims with the evidence stated in the passage.","R1.6":"The delegation most directly targets sentence- and paragraph-level weakness, including tense inconsistency, long run-on sentences, and imprecise phrasing.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unproven stability, robustness, and benchmark generality.","RX.2":"The passage contains potentially broad claims about robustness, real-time deployment, and advantages over alternatives that require restraint.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not rewriting or evaluating the artifact beyond the closed responsibility space.","RX.4":"Overstating controller reliability or deployment readiness could create downstream technical harm if treated as validated control-system evidence.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the artifact, such as the 12-plant benchmark, 23% error reduction, and assumed stability."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the contribution, framing, and technical point of the section.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a multi-step argument about prediction error, online retuning, tracking performance, and robustness that would need coherent causal logic.
rationale.R1.3: Several performance and robustness claims rely on reported benchmark results, so strengthening requires aligning claims with the evidence stated in the passage.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation most directly targets sentence- and paragraph-level weakness, including tense inconsistency, long run-on sentences, and imprecise phrasing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unproven stability, robustness, and benchmark generality.
rationale.RX.2: The passage contains potentially broad claims about robustness, real-time deployment, and advantages over alternatives that require restraint.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not rewriting or evaluating the artifact beyond the closed responsibility space.
rationale.RX.4: Overstating controller reliability or deployment readiness could create downstream technical harm if treated as validated control-system evidence.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the artifact, such as the 12-plant benchmark, 23% error reduction, and assumed stability.
